IN DEPT. 302, TO PROVIDE THE DEFENDANTS AN OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD - REDACTED IS GRANTED. THE PARTIAL AND FINAL ARBITRATION AWARDS ARE CONFIRMED. THE PROPOSED JUDGMENT SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFFS SHALL BE ENTERED, EXCEPT FOR THE STATEMENT ABOUT ATTORNEY'S FEES. PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 1032(A)(4), THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE IS NO "PREVAILING PARTY" AS TO THESE MOTIONS, AS THE MOTIONS ARE LARGELY UNOPPOSED. BOTH PARTIES WERE IN FAVOR OF THE MOTIONS BEING GRANTED. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JON DOYLE, CHARLOTTE DOYLE'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS PENDING ARBITRATION (AMENDED). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. DEFENDANTS ARE PARTIES TO ARBITRATION AND ALSO PARTIES TO THE CLAIMS BROUGHT BY MS. GAROFOLO IN THIS COURT. THERE IS A RISK OF INCONSISTENT RULINGS BECAUSE MS. GAROFOLO'S CLAIMS ARE INTERTWINED WITH THE CLAIMS PROCEEDING IN ARBITRATION. STAYING THE CASE PENDING THE OUTCOME OF THE ARBITRATION SERVES JUDICIAL ECONOMY AND THERE IS NO REAL PREJUDICE TO MS. GAROFOLO IF THE CASE IS STAYED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA, CSR #5153

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SYLVIA GAROFOLO'S MOTION TO LIFT STAY OF PROCEEDINGS - PER ORDER OF THE PRESIDING JUDGE, THIS MOTION IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 5, 2013 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302 TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE KAHN. JUDGE MILLER RECUSES HERSELF. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SYLVIA GAROFOLO'S MOTION TO LIFT STAY OF PROCEEDINGS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED AS SET FORTH HEREIN. THE ONLY CHANGED CIRCUMSTANCES SINCE THE STAY ORDER IS THE FAILURE OF BOTH PLAINTIFF JOSEPH GAROFOLO AND DEFENDANT JON DOYLE TO INITIATE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS. BASED ON THE POSITIONS TAKEN BY MR. DOYLE ON THIS MOTION, MR. DOYLE HAS WAIVED ANY DEFENSES HE MIGHT HAVE TO ANY CLAIMS ASSERTED BY MR. GAROFOLO ON THE GROUNDS THAT MR. GAROFOLO HAS NOT MADE SUFFICIENT EFFORTS TO RESOLVE THE PARTIES' DISPUTES PRIOR TO INITIATION OF ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS. IF MR. GAROFOLO WISHES TO PURSUE HIS CLAIMS AGAINST MR. DOYLE, MR. GAROFOLO SHOULD PROCEED FORTHWITH TO INITIATE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS AND IN, ALL EVENTS, MUST DO SO NO LATER THAN MAY 31, 2013 AND SUCH PROCEEDINGS MUST CONCLUDE NO LATER THAN MAY 31, 2014. MR. AND MRS. DOYLE MUST NOT TAKE ANY ACTION OR OMIT TO TAKE ANY ACTION THAT WOULD MAKE IT DIFFICULT OR IMPOSSIBLE TO MEET THESE DEADLINES. IF MR. GAROFOLO DOES NOT INITIATE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS AS REQUIRED OR THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS DO NOT CONCLUDE AS REQUIRED, MS. PASTOR MAY REFILE HER MOTION. Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award - Redacted
Off Calendar Jan-12-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS JOSEPH GAROFOLO AND SYLVIA GAROFOLO'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is granted. The Partial and Final Arbitration Awards are confirmed. The proposed judgment submitted by Plaintiffs shall be entered, except for the statement about attorney's fees. Pursuant to CCP sec. 1032(a)(4), the Court finds that there is no "prevailing party" as to these motions, as the motions are largely unopposed. Both parties were in favor of the motions being granted. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Interim Arbitration Decision And Award
Off Calendar Jan-12-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH GAROFOLO'S Motion to Seal Interim Arbitration Decision and Award is granted. The Interim Arbitration Decision and Award shall be sealed. Plaintiffs' proposed redacted Interim Arbitration Decision and Award shall be the public version. The Court finds that an overriding interest exists that overcomes the right of public access to the record, the overriding interest supports sealing the records, a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced, the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored, and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. (CRC 2.550(d).) Defendants' opposition and request to redact more of the document does not establish the five factors set forth in CRC 2.550(d). Further, Plaintiffs establish in their reply brief that the majority of the information that Defendants seek to redact is already in the public record. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-05-2013 Continued To Sep-18-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Motion To Lift Stay Of Proceedings
Off Calendar Apr-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ALI ARI AALAEI, ESQ., 415.357.3600, FOR PLAINTIFF SYLVIA GAROFOLO.
ROBERT CHARLES WARD, ESQ., 415.421.6500, FOR PLAINTIFF JOSEPH GAROFOLO.
PATRICK J. CONTI, ESQ., 415.471.3109, FOR DEFENDANT JON DOYLE.

RULING: COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SEEKS CLARIFICATION OF TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SYLVIA GAROFOLO'S Motion To Lift Stay Of Proceedings is denied without prejudice to being refiled as set forth herein. The only changed circumstances since the stay order is the failure of both plaintiff Joseph Garofolo and defendant Jon Doyle to initiate arbitration proceedings. Based on the positions taken by Mr. Doyle on this motion, Mr. Doyle has waived any defenses he might have to any claims asserted by Mr. Garofolo on the grounds that Mr. Garofolo has not made sufficient efforts to resolve the parties' disputes prior to initiation of arbitration proceedings. If Mr. Garofolo wishes to pursue his claims against Mr. Doyle, Mr. Garofolo should proceed forthwith to initiate arbitration proceedings and in, all events, must do so no later than May 31, 2013 and such proceedings must conclude no later than May 31, 2014. Mr. and Mrs. Doyle must not take any action or omit to take any action that would make it difficult or impossible to meet these deadlines. If Mr. Garofolo does not initiate arbitration proceedings as required or the arbitration proceedings do not conclude as required, Ms. Pastor may refile her motion. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: HAROLD KAHN, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-01-2012 Continued To Dec-19-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Jon Doyle, Charlotte Doyle Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Arb (Amended)
Off Calendar May-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS JON DOYLE, CHARLOTTE DOYLE'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS PENDING ARBITRATION (Amended).
Appearances: Robert Charles Ward of Shartsis Friese LLP (421.6500) for plaintiff and responding party; Jaime M. Huling Delaye of Arnold & Porter LLP (471.3115) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Defendants are parties to arbitration and also parties to the claims brought by Ms. Garofolo in this Court. There is a risk of inconsistent rulings because Ms. Garofolo's claims are intertwined with the claims proceeding in arbitration. Staying the case pending the outcome of the arbitration serves judicial economy and there is no real prejudice to Ms. Garofolo if the case is stayed. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153
